question_text: A kettle has a total energy input of 3000 J. It wastes 600 J as heat through its sides. A student improves the kettle's insulation. After insulation, only 150 J is wasted. Calculate the change in energy efficiency as a percentage.
explanation: Before insulation: useful output = 3000 - 600 = 2400 J, so efficiency = (2400/3000) × 100% = 80%. After insulation: useful output = 3000 - 150 = 2850 J, so efficiency = (2850/3000) × 100% = 95%. The change is 95% - 80% = 15% increase.
